campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 19, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council held a meeting on May 19, 2026 where they heard council reports and public comments on staffing vacancies, discussed recruitment and retention efforts for city positions, and announced the Boogie Festival happening that weekend.
keyTopics: Staffing vacancies and recruitment/retention efforts
Public hearing on Government Code Section 3502 procedures
Boogie Festival event this weekend

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — May 20, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council is moving forward with identifying certain properties and has scheduled a public hearing for June 17, 2026, where residents can voice concerns or objections before the city makes a final decision.
keyTopics: Council passed resolution to identify specific properties
Public hearing scheduled for June 17, 2026
Residents can submit protests before final approval

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — June 2, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council met on June 2, 2026, to approve meeting minutes, appoint a youth commissioner, and set up a municipal election for November 3, 2026. The council also approved a new housing development map, authorized several service agreements with vendors, and handled budget matters.
keyTopics: November 3, 2026 municipal election scheduled
Youth Commissioner appointment ratified
Tract 10675 development map approved
New vendor agreements authorized (Accela software and building plan services)
Budget authorization for revenue and expenditures

san-jose.title: San José City Council — June 9, 2026
san-jose.summary: San José City Council met on June 9, 2026, to discuss several budget and facilities matters, including a proposed increase to library parcel tax rates for the upcoming fiscal year and approval for free use of city spaces for the Neighborhoods Conference. A planned vote on the Market Street Garage Improvements Project was postponed to the following week.
keyTopics: Library parcel tax rate increase for 2026-2027
Free use of Council Chambers and plazas for 4th Annual Neighborhoods Conference
Market Street Garage Improvements Project (vote delayed to June 16)
Consultant services agreement with CSG Advisors

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — June 4, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on June 4, 2026, that included both in-person and virtual participation options. Residents could join online via Zoom or phone to watch the meeting and provide public comment during agenda items.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access available via Zoom and phone dial-in
Public comment procedures for online participants
Hybrid meeting format combining in-person and remote attendance

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale Personnel Board — June 8, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Personnel Board held a meeting on June 8, 2026, to review past meeting minutes and recognize the service of board member Jeff Kucer. The meeting included a flag salute and time for public comments.
keyTopics: Recognition of Board Member Jeff Kucer's service
Approval of Personnel Board meeting minutes from August 18, 2025
Public comment period

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — June 2, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to officially recognize June as LGBTQ+ Pride Month and acknowledge the United Nations World Environment Day on June 5, 2026. The council also reserved time for residents to speak about any city matters not listed on the agenda.
keyTopics: LGBTQ+ Pride Month proclamation for June
United Nations World Environment Day recognition
Public comment period for non-agenda items

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — June 9, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a hybrid meeting on June 9, 2026, allowing residents to participate either in person or remotely via Zoom. The council provided multiple ways for the public to submit comments and stay informed about city business.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format available in-person and via Zoom
Remote participation options for residents who cannot attend in person
Written public comment submission through eComment system
Phone dial-in access for Zoom participation

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to approve routine administrative items and discuss updates to the city's federal housing and community development funding plan for 2025-2026. The council also reviewed requests from individual members for new projects to work on and considered changes to how council vacancies are filled.
keyTopics: Updates to federal housing and community development funding plan (Consolidated Plan)
New project requests submitted by individual council members
Changes to rules for filling council vacancies
Approval of previous meeting minutes and confirmation of upcoming meeting schedule

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 7, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council discussed raising storm and surface water drainage fees by 3% for the next fiscal year, bringing the monthly charge to $18.14 per household. The council also discussed state rules around building electrification and potential city subsidies to help residents switch away from natural gas.
keyTopics: Storm water drainage fee increase of 3% ($18.14/month per household)
State electrification regulations and local implementation
Potential city subsidies for building electrification
Rate adjustment reflecting cost-of-living changes
